fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Colorado Pharmacy Technician License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to Colorado - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacy technician license reciprocity colorado, how to transfer pharmacy technician license to colorado, pharmacy technician compact colorado, colorado pharmacy technician endorsement application

board.name: Colorado Board of Pharmacy
board.parent_dept: Colorado Department of Regulatory Agencies (DORA), Division of Professions and Occupations
board.mailing_address: Colorado Department of Regulatory Agencies, Division of Professions and Occupations, 1560 Broadway, Suite 1350, Denver, CO 80202

license_types.title: Certified Pharmacy Technician (CPhT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacy technician in Colorado.

steps.title: 1. Review Application Requirements
steps.description: Visit the Colorado State Board of Pharmacy's Pharmacy Technician Application Information page (https://dpo.colorado.gov/Pharmacy/ApplicationsAndForms#pt) to understand the full eligibility requirements, including age, education/certification (PTCB or NHA), and criminal history guidelines.

steps.title: 2. Create a DORA Online Account
steps.description: Access the Colorado DORA Online Licensing System (https://apps.colorado.gov/dora/licensing/ApplicantSearch/ApplicationTypeSearch.aspx?professionId=100063) and click 'Register a New User Account' to establish your personal online profile.

steps.title: 3. Complete the Online Application
steps.description: Log in to your newly created account, select 'Apply for a New License/Registration', then choose 'Pharmacy Technician' and select 'Initial Application by Examination or Endorsement'. Fill out all required sections of the application accurately.

steps.title: 4. Pay the Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee of $30.00 directly through the online portal using a credit card as part of the application submission process.

steps.title: 5. Submit Supporting Documents
steps.description: Upload all necessary supporting documentation through the online portal or mail them if specified. This typically includes proof of national certification (e.g., PTCB or NHA certification), a completed Criminal History Background Check Authorization form, an Affidavit of Eligibility, and verification of lawful presence.

steps.title: 6. Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check the status of your application via the DORA Online Licensing System by logging into your account. Respond promptly to any requests for additional information or clarification from the Colorado State Board of Pharmacy.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

provisional_text: Colorado offers a temporary or trainee pharmacy technician permit that allows new applicants to begin working in a pharmacy setting under direct pharmacist supervision while completing their certification requirements. Contact the Colorado Board of Pharmacy for specific temporary permit restrictions and duration.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacy technicians registered in another state may apply for registration in Colorado by submitting a new application to the Colorado Board of Pharmacy. There is no interstate compact for pharmacy technicians. Applicants must provide proof of current national certification (PTCB or ExCPT), complete any Colorado-specific requirements, and pass a criminal background check. Processing typically takes 3 to 5 Business Days.

faqs.question: How much does a Colorado pharmacy technician license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Colorado have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Colorado. To work in Colorado, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Colorado?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Colorado while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacy technicians registered in another state may apply for registration in Colorado by submitting a new application to the Colorado Board of Pharmacy. There is no interstate compact for pharmacy technicians. Applicants must provide proof of current national certification (PTCB or ExCPT), complete any Colorado-specific requirements, and pass a criminal background check. Processing typically takes 3 to 5 Business Days.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacy Technician in Colorado
hero.dek: Apply online through the Colorado DORA Online Licensing System (apps.colorado.gov/dora/licensing). Create an account, select 'Pharmacy Technician - Initial Application', and submit your application along with supporting documents.
